data.title: Aya Gold & Silver 1Q Output Jumps as Morocco Operations Ramp UpCommodity Comment
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

Aya Gold & Silver's production of silver nearly tripled in the first quarter as the company continues its ramp-up of operations at its mine in Morocco, and said it has now moved on to producing the more lucrative semi-pure alloy of silver and gold.

On production:

Silver production came to 1.07 million ounces in the first quarter of the year, up from 366,362 ounces a year earlier.

Aya Gold is increasing its operations at the Zgounder Silver Mine, processing 249,743 metric tons of ore, up from 81,331 tons in the prior year.

Vice President of Operations Raphael Beaudoin said the strong performance comes despite planned shutdowns and seasonal rainfall thanks to increased mine and processing rates.

"The plant is currently operating at design capacity and above, with room for improvement," Beaudoin said.

On outlook:

Aya expects to see recovery improvements throughout the year as plant operations are optimized and as more fresh rock is introduced into the plant.

January was the last month in which silver concentrate was produced at Zgounder, said the company. All produced silver is now silver dore, increasing revenue per ounce produced by about 15% compared with the price of concentrate, Aya added.

Silver dore is a mixed alloy of silver and gold that is often produced during the initial stages of refining precious metals.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com

data.title: Done.ai Group AB signs agreement to acquire Nordic digital agency We Assist to expand SME offering across CRM, websites, and marketing
data.description:
24SevenOffice Group AB

Done.ai Group AB ("Done.ai") today signed an agreement to acquire We Assist, a high-growth digital agency and CRM expert with operations in Norway (We Assist AS) and Denmark (We Assist ApS), specializing in website and e-commerce development, performance marketing and international scaling.

This acquisition marks another important step in Done.ai’s journey to become the go-to one-stop shop for businesses, combining smart technology and hands-on services across the full business lifecycle. By integrating We Assist’s expertise in CRM, website development, and marketing automation, Done.ai further strengthens its ability to offer businesses everything they need, from digital visibility to operational tools, in a single, unified platform. With We Assist onboard, Done.ai significantly expands its presence in digital marketing and CRM, two critical pillars for SME success across the Nordics.

“We Assist has built an impressive business with a clear understanding of SME needs, particularly in digital strategy and execution,” said Staffan Herbst, CEO of Done.ai. “Bringing them into the Done.ai platform strengthens our ability to deliver intelligent tools that help businesses look better, work smarter, and grow faster.”

We Assist founder and CEO Kim Kaasene will join Done.ai’s leadership team as Commercial Director, responsible for scaling Done.ai across all markets.

“I’m excited to join forces with Done.ai to take both We Assist and Done.ai`s vision to the next level,” said Kim Kaasene. “We’ve always focused on giving SMEs access to tools and strategies typically reserved for larger companies - and with Done.ai’s platform, we can now do that at scale. With We Assist’s established international presence, Done.ai will be well-positioned to accelerate the rollout of its platform and services across the Nordic region, supported by We Assist’s expertise in digital scaling and go-to-market execution. ”

Transaction Details

Done.ai Group AB has entered into an agreement to acquire 100% of the shares in We Assist AS for a total consideration of NOK 15.8 million, where NOK 5.3 million in cash to be paid at closing and a NOK 10.5 million Seller Credit which can be settled in either cash or shares by 31 August 2025.

We Assist AS owns 100% of the shares in its Danish subsidiary, We Assist ApS, meaning the transaction includes full ownership of both the Norwegian and Danish operations.

The transaction is subject to customary conditions including final board approval and successful completion of financial and operational due diligence.

Financial Performance and Outlook

 

We Assist generated revenues of approximately NOK 10 million in FY2024, with an EBITDA of approximately NOK 1 million, representing a solid and scalable business model supported by recurring customers and digital delivery capacity. With established operations in both Norway and Denmark, the company is well positioned for continued regional expansion.

Under Done.ai’s ownership, We Assist is expected to accelerate its growth, benefiting from the Group’s broader infrastructure, customer base, and technology platform. The transaction supports Done.ai’s ambition to build a fully integrated digital operating system for small businesses across the Nordics.

About Done.ai

 

Done.ai has entered a new chapter marking its transformation from a traditional ERP vendor into a one-stop shop for modern businesses, offering an integrated suite of AI-powered tools that span the full operational workflow. With a modular, API-first architecture, businesses can manage their entire value chain, from first customer touchpoint to back-end accounting, fully integrated in one automated, end-to-end platform.

Through the integration of embedded financial services such as automated treasury management, payment solutions, spend management, and open banking, Done.ai aims to redefine how businesses manage liquidity and financial operations. These services will initially be launched through an exclusive three-year distribution agreement to 24SevenOffice’s extensive customer base, ensuring rapid rollout and adoption.

Combining deep technological expertise, strategic partnerships, and AI-driven automation, Done.ai delivers real-time financial control, operational efficiency, and unmatched scalability, positioning the company as a leading AI-native fintech platform for the business sector.

Done.ai is headquartered in Sweden and listed on Nasdaq First North Growth Market under the ticker 247. The ticker is expected to change to "DONE" following the general meeting on April 10 2025.

About We Assist

 

We Assist is a Nordic digital agency with operations in Norway and Denmark. The company delivers strategic and operational services in CRM systems, website development – including e-commerce, performance marketing and international scaling tailored to the needs of small and medium-sized businesses. With a strong client base and a results-driven approach to digital growth, We Assist is a trusted partner for businesses looking to scale their digital presence. Kasper Bisgaard, who currently serves as marketing director, will assume the role of CEO of We Assist.

 

For further information please contact:

Staffan Herbst, CEO and CFO

Tel: +46 10 490 07 00, ir@done.ai

Certified Adviser

The Certified Adviser to 24SevenOffice on Nasdaq First North Growth Market is Partner Fondkommission.

Address: Lilla Nygatan 2, 411 09 Gothenburg

Telephone: +46 (0)31-761 22 30

Website: partnerfk.com

data.title: Unity Bancorp Inc reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Unity Bancorp Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of $1.13​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 93 cents. The mean expectation of three analysts for the quarter was for earnings of $1.13 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from $1.11 to $1.14 per share.

  • Revenue rose 12.5% to $28.04 million from a year ago; analysts expected $28.82 million.

  • Unity Bancorp Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was $1.13​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $11.6 million.

  • Unity Bancorp Inc shares had fallen by 2.9% this quarter and lost 9.4% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 6% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 3 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the banks peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Unity Bancorp Inc is $54.00

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data April 11 at 11:35 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Low density polyethylene (LDPE) export prices down adjust in the United States
data.description: This week, LDPE export prices drifted lower in the US.An industry source in the U.S. informed a Polymerupdate team member, "US spot export prices for low-density polyethylene (LDPE) experienced a slight decline in the week ending, as market participants noted a slowdown in demand amid evolving global trade conditions. Export distributors reported a significant increase in order cancellations, especially from clients in Asia and the European Union, who are exhibiting heightened caution and hesitance in the market. The sentiment among distributors and buyers has progressively weakened throughout the first quarter, primarily due to increasing uncertainty surrounding tariffs. This instability has considerably affected the demand outlook for US polyethylene for the remainder of 2025, prompting buyers to rethink their sourcing strategies. Market sources indicate that a large share of US polyethylene exports is typically sent to the EU and China. Data from the US International Trade Commission supports this observation, revealing that just over 25% of total polyethylene exports are allocated to these two regions. Consequently, the rising apprehension in these critical export markets is significantly influencing the overall performance of US LDPE.FAS Houston LDPE export prices were assessed at USD 1050-1070/mt, down USD (-10/mt) from the previous week.Spot upstream ethylene prices on Thursday were assessed at the 19.00-19.50 cents/lb FOB U.S. Gulf levels, a sharp week on week drop of (-3 cents/lb).The US ethylene contract price for March 2025 settled at the 35.25 cents/lb mark. This price represents a drop of 1.50 cents/lb from its February 2025 settlement levels."

data.title: ArgenX rises on FDA approval for self-administered Vyvgart syringe
data.description:
** Shares in ArgenX are up 3.7% after the Belgian biotech company received FDA approval for syringe version of its Vyvgart drug

** U.S.-listed shares of the company also rise 5.1% to $574 premarket

** The pre-filled syringe (PFS) will be self-admin permitted with no specific training nor monitoring required

** "This achievement is expected to get more new patients on drug and help ArgenX achieve its 2030 vision with 50k patients on drug," Degroof Petercam says

** "We see the approval as the next phase in maintaining Vyvgart's launch momentum," KBC analysts say

** Both brokers see around 4% price increase for PFS, which KBC says "should facilitate smooth access under Medicare Part D"

** Brokerage William Blair says "Patients could increase the frequency of dosing given the convenience of self-administration to achieve more continuous symptom relief"

** Jefferies expects ARGX will see limited impact from tariffs given U.S. manufacturing footprint from its contract drug manufacturer Lonza

** Up to the previous session's close, the shares were down 18.6%​ YTD​

data.title: Mitsui Fudosan Logistics Park to Buy 50% Stake in Logistics Facility; to Divest Two Industrial Properties
data.description:
Mitsui Fudosan Logistics Park agreed to acquire a 50% interest in Saitama's logistic facility iMissions Park for 3.02 billion yen, according to a Tokyo bourse filing on Thursday.

The company also agreed to divest two industrial properties, namely, T&B Maintenance

Center Toyama and T&B Maintenance Center Matsue, for 2.15 billion yen, and 1.65 billion yen, respectively, the filing said.

data.title: i-80 Gold President and COO to Step Down
data.description:
i-80 Gold said Friday that Matthew Gili is stepping down as president and COO.

The company said Gili will remain in the roles until a successor is appointed and following a transitionary period.

Gili has served as i-80's president and COO since the company's inception in 2021.

Richard Young, i-80's CEO, will take over as president after Gili's departure.

"We are grateful to Matt for his committed leadership and the significant contributions he has made during his tenure with the company," Young said. "He has played a key role in advancing the Company's new development plan to build a mid-tier gold producer in Nevada."

I-80's share price gained 4.1% on Thursday to $0.76 on the TSX.

data.title: Berenberg Keeps JD Sports at Buy After Fiscal Q4 Results, Share Buyback Plans
data.description:
Berenberg maintained JD Sports Fashion's (JD.L) buy rating after the British retailer's fiscal fourth-quarter results came in better than expected, with adjusted pretax profit guidance for fiscal 2025 remaining unchanged.

"There is no significant change to our low-end EPS estimates this year (FY 2025E) or next year (FY 2026E). We see FY 2027E (ending January 2027) as a recovery year for sales (+1.8% lfl), with EBIT supported by efficiency benefits. In FY 2027E, the full benefit of the (L)100m share buybacks planned for FY 2026E should come through," analysts said in a Thursday note. "The prospect of total sales growth at around 5% in an average year over the medium term looks attractive to us, in the light of the very low forward P/E of c6x to January 2026E."

However, analysts also noted that potential tariff impacts remain a concern as the company generates 22% of its sales in the US and sources a substantial portion of its goods from several Southeast Asian countries.

The price target of 1.28 pounds sterling on the stock was retained.

data.title: Shareholders that Lost Money on AppLovin Corporation (APP) Urged to Join Class Action - Contact Levi & Korsinsky to Learn More
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / April 11, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your AppLovin Corporation investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/applovin-corporation-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=142413&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

<img height="0" src="https://app.accessnewswire.com/api/iframe.ashx?releaseid=1013686&url=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fembed%2flBmSeuem6_I" width="0"/></div></div></div><p><strong>THE LAWSUIT:</strong> A class action securities lawsuit was filed against AppLovin Corporation that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between May 10, 2023 and February 25, 2025.</p><p><strong>CASE DETAILS:</strong> According to the complaint, defendants provided investors with material information concerning AppLovin's financial growth and stability. Defendants' statements included, among other things, confidence in AppLovin's launch of its AXON 2.0 digital ad platform and using "cutting-edge AI technologies" to more efficiently match advertisements to mobile games, in addition to expanding into web-based marketing and e-commerce. Moreover, defendants publicly reported impressive financial results, outlooks, and guidance to investors, all while using dishonest advertising practices.</p><p>The truth emerged on February 26, 2025, when analyst research reports emerged stating that AppLovin was reverse engineering and exploiting advertising data from Meta Platforms. The reports further alleged AppLovin was utilizing manipulative practices to artificially inflate their own ad click-through and app download rates, such as by having ads click on themselves or utilizing design gimmicks to trigger forced shadow downloads, erroneously inflating installation numbers and, in turn, its profit figures.</p><p>Following this news, the price of AppLovin's stock declined from $377.06 per share on February 25, 2025 to $331.00 per share on February 26, 2025.</p><p><strong>WHAT'S NEXT?</strong> If you suffered a loss in AppLovin stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to <a href="https://pr.report/9c3i" rel="nofollow">https://zlk.com/pslra-1/applovin-corporation-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=142413&wire=1</a> to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. <strong>There is no cost or obligation to participate.</strong></p><p><strong>WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY:</strong> Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.</p><p><strong>CONTACT:</strong><br/>Levi & Korsinsky, LLP<br/>Joseph E. THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Canopy Growth Corporation that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between May 30, 2024 and February 6, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (i) Canopy had incurred significant costs producing Claybourne pre-rolled joints in connection with the Claybourne product launch in Canada; (ii) the foregoing costs, in addition to certain indirect costs that Canopy incurred in connection with its Storz & Bickel vaporizer devices, were likely to have a significant negative impact on the Company's gross margins and overall financial results; (iii) accordingly, defendants had overstated the efficacy of Canopy's cost reduction measures and the health of its gross margins while downplaying issues with the same; and (iv) as a result, defendants' public statements were materially false and misleading at all relevant times.

data.title: The Greenbrier Companies, Inc. Being Investigated on Behalf of The Greenbrier Companies, Inc. Investors. Contact Levi & Korsinsky For Details
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / April 11, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of The Greenbrier Companies, Inc. ("The Greenbrier Companies, Inc.") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

Greenbrier reported its fiscal second quarter 2025 financial results on April 7, 2025. Among other items, Greenbrier reported non-GAAP earnings per share of $1.69, missing consensus estimates by $0.09, and revenues of $762.1 million, missing consensus estimates by $136.43 million and representing a year-over-year decline of 11.7%. Greenbrier also announced its "decision to close a facility in Romania," citing "market conditions and a comprehensive analysis in Europe[.]"

Following this news, Greenbrier's stock price fell over 11% on April 8, 2025. To obtain additional information, go to:

data.title: Lost Money on Merck & Co., Inc.(MRK)? Join Class Action Suit Seeking Recovery - Contact Levi & Korsinsky
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / April 11, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Merck & Co., Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/merck-co-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=142428&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

<img height="0" src="https://app.accessnewswire.com/api/iframe.ashx?releaseid=1013701&url=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fembed%2fJL_llnRn2HU" width="0"/></div></div></div><p><strong>THE LAWSUIT:</strong> A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Merck & Co., Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between February 3, 2022 and February 3, 2025.</p><p><strong>CASE DETAILS:</strong> According to the complaint, defendants provided investors with material information concerning Merck's expected revenue of $11 billion from sales of Gardasil by 2030. Defendants' statements included, among other things, confidence in Merck's purported ability to utilize successful consumer activation and education efforts on the benefits of Gardasil in order to drive demand and capitalize on eligible populations for vaccination, resulting in confidently optimistic reports and forecasts of Gardasil's growth in China.<br/><br/>The full truth finally emerged on February 4, 2025, when Merck announced it would no longer achieve the long-forecasted $11 billion in sales of Gardasil by 2030, as it would cease shipments of Gardasil to China "through at least midyear" to facilitate a "rapid reduction of inventory." Defendants claimed this was necessitated by the continued over-inflation of overall channel inventories as demand in China for Gardasil had "not recovered to the level we had expected."</p><p>Following this news, Merck's common stock declined dramatically. From a closing market price of $99.79 per share on February 3, 2025, Merck's stock price fell to $90.74 per share on February 4, 2025, a decline of more than 9% in the span of just a single day.</p><p><strong>WHAT'S NEXT?</strong> If you suffered a loss in Merck stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to <a href="https://pr.report/9c5s" rel="nofollow">https://zlk.com/pslra-1/merck-co-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=142428&wire=1</a> to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. <strong>There is no cost or obligation to participate.</strong></p><p><strong>WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY:</strong> Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.</p><p><strong>CONTACT:</strong></p><p>Levi & Korsinsky, LLP<br/>Joseph E. data.title: Croatia plans to raise target for renewable energy use
data.description:
ZAGREB (Croatia), April 11 (SeeNews) - The Croatian government has adopted a draft law to increase the national target for the share of renewable energy in gross final energy consumption, to be achieved by the end of 2029, economy minister Ante Susnjar said.

"Until 2030, at least 42.5% of the total energy consumption of Croatia would come from renewable energy, increasing the target from the current level of 36.6% and confirming out strategic direction in energy transition," Susnjar told a weekly cabinet meeting on Thursday, as heard in an audio recording of the sitting published on the government website.

The proposed new target is ambitious but achievable, Susnjar added. For the purpose, Croatia needs to amend its law on renewable energy sources and highly efficient energy generation in line with EU directives.

Under the proposal, a new regulation for the consumption of self-supplied renewable energy by households and companies will be introduced as of January 1, 2026, as Croatia was granted a two-year postponement of the implementation of EU legislation that no longer allow self-supply customers to be subsidized through non-payment of network fees and other charges when taking electricity from the grid.

According to the latest available data published in December by Eurostat, renewable energy represented 28.05% of all energy consumed in Croatia in 2023 and 28.09% in 2022.

data.title: Suedzucker Expects Significant Drop in Earnings
data.description:
By Adam Whittaker

German sugar company Suedzucker said it expects earnings for the first quarter to fall.

Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization should be significantly below the 230 million euros ($257.6 million) reported in the prior-year period, it said Friday.

Operating profit for the quarter--which covers March 1 to May 31--is also expected to fall significantly from 155 million euros.

Suedzucker confirmed guidance issued in mid-February, forecasting a moderate decline in group revenues for the fiscal year. EBITDA is seen at between 525 million and 675 million euros while operating profit is expected at 150 million to 300 million euros.

Its forecast is based on rising European sugar prices, which should boost earnings, it said. However, there is still considerable instability stemming from the Ukraine war and global tariffs that clouds the outlook, the company added.

In March, the company said fiscal 2025 revenue was around 9.7 billion euros, while EBITDA decreased to about 715 million euros. Operating profit was around 340 million euros.

Shares were up 0.6% at 11.07 euros in European midday trading.

Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com

data.title: JPMorgan's Dimon warns economy is facing 'considerable turbulence' from tariffs and trade wars
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase & Co.'s stock (JPM) rose 3.8% in premarket trading Friday after the bank said its first-quarter profit rose to $14.64 billion or $5.07 a share, from $13.42 billion, or $4.44 a share in the year-ago quarter. The U.S.'s largest bank beat the FactSet Consensus estimate of $4.63 for its first-quarter profit. First-quarter revenue rose to $45.3 billion from $41.93 billion in the year-ago quarter, and beat the analyst estimate of $43.99 billion. JPMorgan Chase's stock has fallen 5.3% in 2025, as of Thursday's closing bell. the S&P 500 is down 10.4% this year, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average is off by 6.9%.

-Steve Gelsi

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.
